title: Hylorhipsalis rugulosa
taxonomy: Hylorhipsalis rugulosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Portulacaceae. It is native to Mexico and is also known as a type of living stone or lithops. Hylorhipsalis rugulosa is a succulent plant that stores water in its leaves and stems, allowing it to thrive in dry environments. The plant gets its common name from its resemblance to rocks, with its flat, stone-like shape and ability to photosynthesize. Hylorhipsalis rugulosa is often cultivated as an ornamental plant due to its unique appearance and ability to thrive in low-maintenance conditions.

intro: Hylorhipsalis rugulosa, also known as the "tree cactus," is a rare and unique epiphytic cactus species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, fever, and skin problems, due to its reported anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Hylorhipsalis rugulosa include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Hylorhipsalis rugulosa is commonly used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory infections, fever, and skin conditions.
